name: Log signal API endpoint
description: Expose parsed log signals via HTTP so the frontend video player can overlay a timeline. Reads the qlog file for the route (smaller than rlog, fine for UI scale), runs it through the cereal parser, and returns a JSON time-series. Cache the parsed result on disk next to the qlog (e.g., signals.json) so repeat requests are fast.
acceptance_criteria: GET /v1/routes/:dongle_id/:route_name/signals returns JSON { times: [unix_ms...], speed_mps: [...], steering_deg: [...], engaged: [bool...], alerts: [string...] } built from concatenated qlogs across all segments of the route (in segment-number order)
First request computes and writes <STORAGE_PATH>/<dongle_id>/<route>/<segment>/signals.json per segment; subsequent requests read the cached JSON if present and the qlog mtime is unchanged
If a segment has no qlog yet, that segment is skipped (not treated as an error) so partial routes still return useful data
Endpoint requires JWT auth via existing middleware
internal/api/signals.go (new) holds the handler; registered in the same place route endpoints are

internal/api/signals_test.go covers: cache miss (computes and writes), cache hit (does not recompute -- verified by deleting the qlog after first call and expecting success), missing qlog across whole route (returns 200 with empty arrays, not 500), 404 on unknown route
go vet ./... and go build ./... pass cleanly
